items.name: Gopi Chandan and Tilak
items.description: Sacred Gopi Chandan for Vaishnav tilak
items.lore: Gopi Chandan is considered sacred clay from Dwarka and is traditionally used by Krishna devotees for applying tilak. The tilak symbolizes surrender to Krishna and reminds devotees to live with devotion and humility.

items.name: Hare Krishna Chant Card
items.lore: The Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is regarded in ISKCON tradition as the simplest path to spiritual joy in Kali Yuga. Keeping the chant close serves as a reminder to reconnect with Krishna through naam jap anytime, anywhere.

items.name: Krishna and Radha Acrylic Stand
items.lore: Radha and Krishna together symbolize divine love, devotion, and spiritual union. Their playful leelas in Vrindavan continue to inspire bhakti traditions across generations.

items.name: Krishna Bhagavad Gita Cards
items.description: Pocket wisdom cards inspired by Bhagavad Gita
items.lore: The Bhagavad Gita captures Krishna's timeless guidance on life, purpose, and inner peace. These cards present simple reflections that bring Krishna's teachings into everyday moments.

items.name: Laddu Gopal Idol and Shringar Set
items.description: Laddu Gopal idol with decorative shringar
items.lore: Laddu Gopal represents Krishna's joyful childhood form. Devotees lovingly care for Bal Gopal with clothes, bhog, and shringar as an expression of affection and seva.

items.name: Makkhan Matki Diya
items.description: Matki-style diya inspired by Krishna's makhan leela
items.lore: Krishna's childhood stories are filled with playful butter-stealing leelas. The makkhan matki symbolizes innocence, joy, and the sweetness of devotion.

items.name: Yamuna Water Spray
items.description: Sacred Yamuna jal spray inspired by Krishna's Vrindavan leelas
items.lore: The Yamuna River holds deep significance in Krishna bhakti traditions and is closely associated with Krishna's childhood leelas in Vrindavan. This sacred water spray brings a refreshing sense of devotion, purity, and spiritual calm into everyday spaces.

items.name: Krishna Metal Figurine
items.description: Metal figurine of Krishna playing flute
items.lore: Krishna's flute is believed to call souls toward divine love and bliss. The image of Krishna playing bansuri in Vrindavan represents peace, attraction, and spiritual harmony.

items.name: Peacock Feather Agarbatti Stand and Dhoop Holder
items.description: Peacock feather incense and dhoop holder
items.lore: The peacock feather is one of Krishna's most beloved symbols, representing beauty, grace, and divine playfulness. Incense offerings create a sacred atmosphere during prayer and meditation.

items.name: Peacock Feather Bookmark with Tassle
items.lore: Peacock feathers are deeply associated with Krishna's crown and Vrindavan leelas. This bookmark brings a touch of devotion and beauty to daily reading and reflection.

items.name: Tulsi Mala
items.description: Sacred Tulsi mala for chanting and devotion
items.lore: Tulsi is deeply revered in Krishna bhakti traditions and is considered especially dear to Lord Krishna. Chanting on a Tulsi mala is believed to purify the heart and deepen devotion.
